{
  "name": "read_file",
  "response": {
    "output": "/**\n * src/services/apiClient.js\n */\nimport { API_REGISTRY } from './apiRegistry';\nimport { ApiError } from './ApiError';\nimport { getFriendlyErrorMessage } from './errorMapper';\n\nconst BASE_URL = import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL;\n\nexport const executeAction = async (actionPath, payload = {}, token = null, options = {}) => {\n  let backendActionString;\n\n  // 1. Resolve the backend action string\n  if (actionPath.includes('.')) {\n    const [domain, actionName] = actionPath.split('.');\n    backendActionString = API_REGISTRY[domain]?.[actionName];\n  } else {\n    // If no dot, assume it's already the resolved action string (e.g. from API_REGISTRY constants)\n    backendActionString = actionPath;\n  }\n\n  if (!backendActionString) {\n    throw new ApiError(`Developer Error: Unregistered API Action: ${actionPath}`);\n  }\n\n  const requestBody = {\n    action: backendActionString,\n    payload: payload \n  };\n\n  if (token) requestBody.token = token;\n\n  // --- Centralized Request Logger ---\n  console.groupCollapsed(`🚀 API Request: [${actionPath}] -> ${backendActionString}`);\n  console.log('Payload:', payload);\n  console.log('Full Request Body:', requestBody);\n  console.groupEnd();\n  // ----------------------------------\n\n  try {\n    const response = await fetch(BASE_URL, {\n      method: 'POST',\n      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain;charset=utf-8' },\n      body: JSON.stringify(requestBody),\n      signal: options.signal\n    });\n\n    // 1. Check for hard network/server crashes (500s, 404s)\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      throw new ApiError(\"Network or server connection failed.\", response.status);\n    }\n\n    // 2. Parse the JSON Envelope\n    const data = await response.json();\n\n    // --- Centralized Response Logger ---\n    console.groupCollapsed(`✅ API Response: [${actionPath}]`);\n    console.log('Status:', response.status);\n    console.log('Data:', data);\n    console.groupEnd();\n    // ----------------------------------\n\n    // 3. Catch logical backend errors (Assuming GAS returns { success: false, error: \"...\" })\n    if (data.success === false || data.status === 'error') {\n      const rawError = data.error || data.message;\n      const friendlyMessage = getFriendlyErrorMessage(rawError, actionPath);\n      \n      throw new ApiError(friendlyMessage, response.status, rawError);\n    }\n\n    return data;\n\n  } catch (error) {\n    // If it's already our custom error, just pass it up\n    if (error instanceof ApiError) throw error;\n    \n    // Otherwise, wrap standard JS/Network errors\n    console.error(`Unexpected API Call Failure [${actionPath}]:`, error);\n    throw new ApiError(\"Unable to reach the server. Please check your internet connection.\", null, error.message);\n  }\n};\n\n/**\n * Standardized API Client Export\n */\nexport const apiClient = {\n  executeAction\n};\n"
  },
  "id": "synth_59f6698b-2493-47dc-bc2e-b018462a8fff_1779044255269_38"
}

## MODEL ✨

**Refining API Integration** I'm reviewing the `apiClient` and have identified a potential lead within `src/features/teacher/hooks/useTeacherQueries.js`. My current focus is on understanding the interaction within the `useTeachersQuery` hook.**Investigating Teacher Fetching** I'm scrutinizing how teacher data is retrieved. Specifically, I'm examining `useTeacherQueries.js` and the `useTeachersQuery` hook to ensure no unintended filters are being applied to the backend when requesting teacher lists, especially considering the `EMPTY_FILTER` scenario and the implications of `init_erp` hydration.**Examining Teacher Queries** I've confirmed that `useTeachersQuery()` is called without arguments, indicating no explicit filtering from that hook. My review of `useTeacherDetailQuery` also reveals no default filters. It seems the filtering logic resides client-side within `useFilteredTeachers`, operating on an `initialTeachers` array.**Uncovering Filtering Logic** I've discovered that if `initialTeachers` is empty, `filteredTeachers` will also be empty. I'm considering whether the `init_erp` response for `Teacher` might already be filtered, and though bypassing hydration is an option for certainty, it's outside the current scope. My attention has shifted to a specific query in `AddBatch.jsx` which seems unrelated to the current page focus.**Checking Teacher Filters** I'm revisiting the `Teachers.jsx` component to see if a status filter might have existed previously, as the current version lacks one. I'm also considering if `TeacherSelectionModal` might be applying filters. I've confirmed `EMPTY_FILTER` is indeed `{}`. I've noted that missing `teacher.status` defaults to 'Active' in `teacherSchema.jsx`. Lastly, I'm verifying if `Teachers.jsx` utilizes the hydration data.**Tool Command**:

}

'primary' : 'secondary'}\n                          className=\"rounded-2xl\"\n                        />\n                        <div className={`size-6 rounded-full border-2 flex items-center justify-center transition-all ${\n                          isSelected ? 'bg-primary border-primary' : 'border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700 group-hover:border-primary/50'\n                        }`}>\n                          {isSelected && <span className=\"material-symbols-outlined text-[14px] text-white font-black\">check</span>}\n                        </div>\n                      </div>\n                      \n                      <div className=\"flex-1\">\n                        <p className={`text-sm font-black transition-colors ${isSelected ? 'text-primary' : 'text-slate-900 dark:text-white'}`}>\n                          {teacher.teacher_name}\n                        </p>\n                        <p className=\"text-xs text-primary font-bold mt-0.5\">{teacher.specialization || 'General'}</p>\n                        \n                        <div className=\"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 mt-3\">\n                          <span className=\"text-[9px] font-black text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-tighter\">{teacher.teacher_id}</span>\n                          <span className=\"size-1 rounded-full bg-slate-300\"></span>\n                          <span className={`text-[9px] font-black uppercase tracking-tighter ${teacher.status === 'active' ? 'text-green-500' : 'text-slate-400'}`}>\n                            {teacher.status}\n                          </span>\n                        </div>\n                      </div>\n\n                      <div className=\"pt-4 border-t border-slate-100 dark:border-slate-800 flex items-center justify-between mt-auto\">\n                        <div className=\"flex flex-col\">\n                          <span className=\"text-[9px] font-black text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-widest leading-none mb-1\">Experience</span>\n                          <p className=\"text-xs font-bold text-slate-900 dark:text-white\">{teacher.experience_years || '0'} Years</p>\n                        </div>\n                        <div className=\"flex flex-col items-end\">\n                          <span className=\"text-[9px] font-black text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-widest leading-none mb-1\">Designation</span>\n                          <p className=\"text-xs font-bold text-slate-900 dark:text-white\">{teacher.designation || 'Faculty'}</p>\n                        </div>\n                      </div>\n                    </button>\n                  );\n                })}\n              </div>\n            ) : (\n              <div className=\"h-full flex flex-col items-center justify-center text-center\">\n                <div className=\"size-24 bg-slate-50 dark:bg-slate-800 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-6\">\n                  <span className=\"material-symbols-outlined text-5xl text-slate-200\">person_off</span>\n                </div>\n                <h4 className=\"text-xl font-bold text-slate-900 dark:text-white\">No teachers found</h4>\n                <p className=\"text-sm text-slate-500 max-w-sm mx-auto mt-2\">We couldn't find any faculty members matching your search or filters.</p>\n              </div>\n            )}\n          </main>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* Modal Footer */}\n        <div className=\"p-6 bg-slate-50 dark:bg-slate-900/80 border-t border-slate-100 dark:border-slate-800 flex items-center justify-between z-10\">\n          <div className=\"flex items-center gap-4\">\n            <div className=\"flex -space-x-3 overflow-hidden\">\n              {tempSelected.slice(0, 5).map((t, i) => (\n                <Avatar \n                  key={i} \n                  name={t.teacher_name} \n                  size=\"sm\" \n                  className=\"ring-4 ring-white dark:ring-slate-900\"\n                />\n              ))}\n              {tempSelected.length > 5 && (\n                <div className=\"inline-block size-10 rounded-full ring-4 ring-white dark:ring-slate-900 bg-slate-100 text-slate-500 flex items-center justify-center font-black text-[10px]\">\n                  +{tempSelected.length - 5}\n                </div>\n              )}\n            </div>\n            <div className=\"flex flex-col\">\n              <span className=\"text-xs font-black text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-widest leading-none mb-1\">\n                {singleSelect ? 'Assignment Confirmed' : 'Faculty Selection'}\n              </span>\n              <p className=\"text-sm font-bold text-slate-900 dark:text-white\">\n                <span className=\"text-primary\">{tempSelected.length}</span> {tempSelected.length === 1 ? 'Teacher' : 'Teachers'} Selected\n              </p>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n          <div className=\"flex gap-3\">\n            <button onClick={onClose} className=\"px-6 py-2.5 text-sm font-bold text-slate-500 hover:text-slate-700 transition-colors\">Cancel</button>\n            <button \n              onClick={handleConfirm}\n              className=\"px-10 py-3 bg-primary text-white rounded-2xl font-black text-xs uppercase tracking-widest shadow-lg shadow-primary/20 hover:bg-primary-dark transition-all active:scale-95 flex items-center gap-3\"\n            >\n              <span className=\"material-symbols-outlined text-lg\">\n                {singleSelect ? 'person_add' : 'group_add'}\n              </span>\n              {singleSelect ? 'Confirm Assignment' : 'Update Selection'}\n            </button>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n};\n\nexport default TeacherSelectionModal;\n"
  },
  "id": "synth_59f6698b-2493-47dc-bc2e-b018462a8fff_1779044269392_39"
}

## MODEL ✨

**Refining Teacher Filtering** I'm focusing on refining the teacher filtering logic. It appears the `TeacherSelectionModal`'s `statusFilter` defaulting to "All" might be contributing to an issue where the frontend isn't correctly reflecting active-only teachers as intended by the GAS script's `init_erp` function. I'm investigating the `useErpHydration.js` mapping for clues.**Adjusting Initialization Fetch** I'm shifting my focus to how initial data is fetched for administrators. It seems the current approach might be inadvertently filtering teacher records during the `init_erp` process, which contradicts the need for admins to see all records. I'm examining `useTeacherQueries.js` to ensure unfiltered teacher data is retrieved when necessary.**Investigating Data Hydration** I'm now delving into the `HYDRATION_CONFIG` within `useErpHydration.js`. I suspect the way query keys are utilized here might be causing implicit filtering, even though no explicit filtering was added in my recent `AddTeacher.jsx` updates. I'm examining this configuration to understand potential unintended data retrieval behavior.**Revisiting Query Dependencies** I'm re-examining `useTeachersQuery` and its configuration, specifically `staleTime: Infinity` and `refetchOnMount: false`. These settings currently mean it relies solely on `init_erp`. If I adjust these, it should trigger a call to `data_query` with an empty `where` clause, potentially fetching all data if the backend is generic. I'm now checking for any other teacher fetching points.**Adjusting Query Filters** I'm now focused on ensuring the `useTeachersQuery` hook, as defined in `useTeacherQueries.js`, isn't inadvertently filtering teacher data. I'm investigating whether passing `EMPTY_FILTER` to the hook is truly resulting in an unfiltered backend request, especially considering the user's emphasis on admins seeing all records.**Discovering Hardcoded Filter** I've identified a hardcoded `status: 'active'` filter within `course.api.js` for fetching courses. This appears to be the root cause of unintended filtering, rather than issues in teacher-specific logic. I'm now confirming if this is the only instance.**Confirming Backend Behavior** I'm now examining the `data_query` API call to confirm how it handles empty `where` clauses, as suggested by the previous thought. I need to ensure that when no filter is explicitly passed, the backend returns all teacher records, which is critical for administrator views.**Confirming Data Fetching** I'm now focused on confirming how the `init_erp` action retrieves data. If this "god" action is applying filters on the backend, it would taint the cache and cause the observed issues. I need to verify the parameters being sent to `init_erp` to ensure it's not inadvertently filtering records.**Tool Command**:

{
  "name": "read_file",
  "response": {
    "output": "# API Specification: `init_erp` (Bulk DSL Hydration)\n\n**Action Name:** `init_erp` (Mapped to `ADMIN.INIT_ERP`)\n**Status:** Finalized\n**Description:** A declarative endpoint designed for app hydration. It allows the frontend to request multiple data tables in a single request, supporting both simple table names and advanced DSL query objects.\n\n---\n\n## Request Payload\n\nThe request expects a `targets` array containing either strings (Simple Mode) or objects (Advanced Mode).\n\n```json\n{\n  \"action\": \"init_erp\",\n  \"payload\": {\n    \"targets\": [\n      \"Course\", \n      { \n        \"target\": \"Student\", \n        \"filter\": { \"status\": \"active\" },\n        \"limit\": 1000\n      }\n    ]\n  },\n  \"token\": \"...\"\n}\n```\n\n### Target Types:\n1. **Simple Mode (String):** e.g., `\"Course\"`. Fetches all active records for that table using default settings.\n2. **Advanced Mode (Object):** e.g., `{ \"target\": \"Student\", ... }`. Allows passing specific DSL parameters like `filter`, `limit`, `offset`, etc.\n\n---\n\n## Response Structure\n\nThe response is an object where each requested target is mapped to a pluralized, lowercased key. Each key contains a nested structure with the record count and the data array.\n\n```json\n{\n  \"success\": true,\n  \"data\": {\n    \"courses\": {\n      \"count\": 150,\n      \"data\": [\n        { \"id\": \"C1\", \"name\": \"Mathematics\", ... },\n        ...\n      ]\n    },\n    \"students\": {\n      \"count\": 1000,\n      \"data\": [\n        { \"id\": \"S1\", \"name\": \"John Doe\", ... },\n        ...\n      ]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Key Mapping Rules:\n- The response key is derived from the `target` name.\n- Transformation: `target.toLowerCase() + 's'` (e.g., `Course` -> `courses`, `Batch` -> `batches`).\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Frontend (React)\n- **Location:** `src/hooks/useErpHydration.js`\n- **Cache Strategy:** Injects the `data` array of each response key directly into the React Query cache using predefined `queryKeys`.\n- **Consistency:** Uses the `queryKeys` factory to ensure cache keys match those used by individual page queries (e.g., `queryKeys.courses.list({})`).\n\n### Backend (GAS)\n- **Dispatcher:** Iterates over the `targets` array.\n- **Query Execution:** Uses the `QueryEngine` to process each target. If a target is a string, it's treated as a `target` property with no extra filters.\n- **Pluralization:** Programmatically generates keys for the response object.\n- **Error Handling:** Returns `success: false` if any of the target queries fail, ensuring the hydration guard can prompt a retry.\n"
  },
  "id": "synth_59f6698b-2493-47dc-bc2e-b018462a8fff_1779044326193_42"
}
```

However, a quick look at the specifications clarified that "Simple Mode" only accepts a single string, implying a need for a more granular approach.**Clarifying Hydration Targets** I'm refining how `init_erp` handles targets. It appears "Simple Mode" defaults to active records, which isn't ideal for the "don't filter teacher" request. I need to switch `Teacher` to "Advanced Mode" in `useErpHydration.js` and ensure an empty filter is passed, or determine if this applies to all targets.**Refining Hydration Logic** I'm currently reconsidering the scope of unfiltered data retrieval for admin sessions. HYDRATION_CONFIG = {\n    'Course': { query_key: queryKeys.course, response_key: 'courses' },\n    'Teacher': { query_key: queryKeys.teacher, response_key: 'teachers' },\n    'Student': { query_key: queryKeys.student, response_key: 'students' },\n    'Batch': { query_key: queryKeys.batch, response_key: 'batches' },\n    'Branch': { query_key: queryKeys.branch, response_key: 'branches' },\n  };\n\n  // 🚀 Construct targets for init_erp\n  // For Teacher, we use Advanced Mode with an empty filter to ensure ALL records are fetched (not just active).\n  // Other targets still use Simple Mode (string) which defaults to 'active' as per API spec.\n  const HYDRATION_TARGETS = [\n    'Course',\n    { target: 'Teacher', filter: {} }, \n    'Student',\n    'Batch',\n    'Branch'\n  ];\n\n  return useQuery({\n    queryKey: ['init_erp', { targets: HYDRATION_TARGETS }],\n    queryFn: async () => {\n      console.log('🚀 Starting ERP Hydration with targets:', HYDRATION_TARGETS);\n\n });\n\n      return data;\n    },\n    enabled: !!token,\n    staleTime: Infinity,\n    gcTime: Infinity,\n    refetchOnWindowFocus: false,\n  });\n};",

'courses' },\n    'Teacher': { query_key: queryKeys.teacher, response_key: 'teachers' },\n    'Student': { query_key: queryKeys.student, response_key: 'students' },\n    'Batch': { query_key: queryKeys.batch, response_key: 'batches' },\n    'Branch': { query_key: queryKeys.branch, response_key: 'branches' },\n  };\n\n  const HYDRATION_TARGETS = Object.keys(HYDRATION_CONFIG);\n\n  return useQuery({\n    queryKey: ['init_erp', { targets: HYDRATION_TARGETS }],\n    queryFn: async () => {\n      console.log('🚀 Starting ERP Hydration with targets:', HYDRATION_TARGETS);\n\n      const response = await apiClient.executeAction(\n        API_REGISTRY.ADMIN.INIT_ERP, \n        { targets: HYDRATION_TARGETS }, \n        token\n      );\n\n      if (!response.success) {\n        throw new Error(response.message || 'Failed to initialize ERP data');\n      }\n\n      const data = response.data || {};\n      const now = Date.now();\n\n      // 🛡️ Dynamic Cache Injection\n      HYDRATION_TARGETS.forEach(targetName => {\n        const config = HYDRATION_CONFIG[targetName];\n        if (!config) return;\n\n        const responseKey = config.response_key;\n        const result = data[responseKey];\n\n        console.log(`🔍 Inspecting hydration for ${targetName} (Response Key: ${responseKey})...`);\n\n        if (result && Array.isArray(result.data)) {\n          const records = result.data;\n          \n          console.log(`💧 Hydrating ${targetName}: ${records.length} records found.`);\n          console.log(`📦 Cache Key:`, JSON.stringify(config.query_key.list(EMPTY_FILTER)));\n\n          queryClient.setQueryData(\n            config.query_key.list(EMPTY_FILTER), \n            records, \n            { updatedAt: now }\n          );\n        } else {\n          console.warn(`⚠️ No records found in response for key: ${responseKey}. Data structure:`, result);\n        }\n      });\n\n      return data;\n    },\n    enabled: !!token,\n    staleTime: Infinity,\n    gcTime: Infinity,\n    refetchOnWindowFocus: false,\n  });\n};"
  },
  "id": "synth_59f6698b-2493-47dc-bc2e-b018462a8fff_1779044354031_43"
}

## MODEL ✨

**Investigating Teacher Filtering** I'm currently examining whether teachers are being filtered on the `Teacher` page or within the `DataTable`. My recent focus has been on analyzing `useErpHydration.js` for potential filtering during fetching and cross-referencing this with the logic in `useTeachersQuery.js`.**Analyzing Teacher Query** I'm delving into `useTeachersQuery.js` to understand its default filter behavior, as the user's previous directive was to avoid initial filtering. My focus is on identifying any implicit or unintended filtering that might still be active, even after the cancellation of the `useErpHydration.js` adjustment.**Confirming Rendering Filters** I've confirmed that the `filteredTeachers` are indeed being passed to the `DataTable` component. My current analysis is focused on the `useFilteredTeachers` hook, specifically the `useMemo` block, to fully understand how the client-side filtering logic operates on the `initialTeachers` data.**Confirming Server-Side Filtering** I've identified that the `init_erp` fetch, when using `"Teacher"`, specifically requests "all **active** records" from the backend. This means the server is filtering by status before the data even reaches my client-side filtering hooks, which only handle search and department.**Adjusting Hydration Mode** I'm now focusing on `useErpHydration.js`, specifically the `HYDRATION_CONFIG`. It appears the current setup defaults to fetching only "active" teachers. I need to switch `Teacher` to "Advanced Mode" to disable this server-side filtering, aligning with the instruction to avoid initial filtering.**Investigating Initial Data** I'm reviewing the context of our prior discussions. It appears there was a request to focus solely on creation APIs and then, separately, an inquiry about rendering filters. My current thought is to determine if the initial data fetched from the backend, possibly via hydration, is already filtered before client-side logic is applied.**Tool Command**:

    "output": "import React from 'react';\nimport { TableContainer, TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell, TableLoading, TableError, TableEmpty } from './table';\n\nconst DataTable = ({\n  title,\n  subtitle,\n  primaryAction,\n  secondaryAction,\n  filters,\n  columns,\n  data = [],\n  isLoading,\n  error,\n  onRetry,\n  emptyMessage = \"No records found.\",\n  emptyIcon = \"person_off\"\n}) => {\n  return (\n    <div className=\"space-y-6\">\n      {/* Header Section */}\n      <div className=\"flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between items-start md:items-center gap-4\">\n        <div>\n          <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-bold text-text-main dark:text-white\">{title}</h1>\n          {subtitle && <p className=\"text-sm text-text-secondary\">{subtitle}</p>}\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"flex items-center gap-3\">\n          {secondaryAction}\n          {primaryAction}\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Filters Section */}\n      {filters && (\n        <div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-4 rounded-xl border border-border-light dark:border-border-dark bg-surface-light dark:bg-surface-dark p-4 shadow-sm\">\n          {filters}\n        </div>\n      )}\n\n      {/* Table Area with Loading/Error states */}\n      <div className=\"rounded-xl border border-border-light dark:border-border-dark bg-surface-light dark:bg-surface-dark shadow-sm overflow-hidden min-h-[400px] flex flex-col\">\n        {isLoading ? (\n          <TableLoading />\n        ) : error ? (\n          <TableError error={error} onRetry={onRetry} />\n        ) : (\n          <TableContainer>\n            <TableHeader>\n              <TableRow className=\"hover:bg-transparent dark:hover:bg-transparent\">\n                {columns.map((col, index) => (\n                  <TableHead key={index} align={col.align} className={col.headerClassName}>\n                    {col.header}\n                  </TableHead>\n                ))}\n              </TableRow>\n            </TableHeader>\n            <TableBody>\n              {data.length === 0 ? (\n                <TableRow className=\"hover:bg-transparent dark:hover:bg-transparent\">\n                  <TableCell colSpan={columns.length} align=\"center\">\n                    <TableEmpty message={emptyMessage} icon={emptyIcon} />\n                  </TableCell>\n                </TableRow>\n              ) : (\n                data.map((row, rowIndex) => (\n                  <TableRow key={row.id || rowIndex}>\n                    {columns.map((col, colIndex) => (\n                      <TableCell key={colIndex} align={col.align} className={col.className}>\n                        {col.cell ? col.cell(row) : col.render ? col.render(row) : (typeof col.accessor === 'function' ? col.accessor(row) : row[col.accessor] || '')}\n                      </TableCell>\n                    ))}\n                  </TableRow>\n                ))\n              )}\n            </TableBody>\n          </TableContainer>\n        )}\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n};\n\nexport default DataTable;\n"
  },
  "id": "synth_59f6698b-2493-47dc-bc2e-b018462a8fff_1779044606715_44"
}

## MODEL ✨

**Refining Filtering Logic** My current focus is on understanding where filtering is actually occurring. I've confirmed `DataTable` itself doesn't handle it; it merely displays the `data` prop. The filtering appears to be split, with a significant part happening server-side within the `init_erp` action, likely due to string targets in `useErpHydration.js`.**Investigating Query Logic** I'm double-checking `useTeacherQueries.js` to ensure no frontend filters are inadvertently applied there. My current hypothesis is that the backend's default fetching logic needs adjustment to accommodate the admin's request for all record types. My next step is to confirm the exact parameters being passed to `apiClient.executeAction`.**Adjusting Hydration Behavior** I'm now focusing on explicitly disabling backend default filters by updating `useErpHydration.js` to leverage "Advanced Mode" for `Teacher` queries with an empty filter object. This should ensure all records are fetched, regardless of any implicit backend configurations. I've also re-verified that `useFilteredTeachers.js` lacks any hardcoded status checks.**Tool Command**:

    "instruction": "Switch 'Teacher' to Advanced Mode in HYDRATION_TARGETS with an explicit empty filter. This bypasses the backend's 'active only' default for string targets, ensuring admins see all records. Also clean up the HYDRATION_CONFIG order for clarity.",
